title: actaea racemosa var. racemosa
intro: Actaea racemosa var. racemosa, also known as American black cohosh, is a perennial flowering plant native to eastern North America. The roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of American black cohosh include relief from menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and night sweats, as well as pain relief and improved sleep quality. American black cohosh is typically prepared as a tincture, capsule, or tea, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
